project: Native SQLite via KV Channel Protocol
description: Replace WASM-based SQLite with a native Rust SQLite binary (napi-rs addon) that routes page-level KV operations over a new WebSocket KV channel protocol. Includes WASM VFS improvements, protocol schema, native addon, server-side endpoints, and integration.

userStories.title: Create KV channel protocol BARE schema (v1.bare)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the KV channel protocol schema defined so that both client and server can serialize/deserialize messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory engine/sdks/schemas/kv-channel-protocol/
Create v1.bare with all types from docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Schema' section (lines 86-233)

Types include: Id, ActorOpenRequest, ActorCloseRequest, ActorOpenResponse, ActorCloseResponse, KvKey, KvValue, KvGetRequest, KvPutRequest, KvDeleteRequest, KvDeleteRangeRequest, RequestData union, ErrorResponse, KvGetResponse, KvPutResponse, KvDeleteResponse, ResponseData union, ToServerRequest, ToServerPong, ToServer union, ToClientResponse, ToClientPing, ToClientClose, ToClient union
Schema follows same file structure as runner-protocol/v7.bare (with MARK comments for sections)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference engine/sdks/schemas/runner-protocol/v7.bare for BARE schema conventions. The KV types mirror runner protocol KV types but omit KvListRequest, KvDropRequest, and KvMetadata.

userStories.title: Create KV channel protocol TypeScript versioned handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript code to handle versioned BARE serialization/deserialization of KV channel protocol messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create engine/sdks/schemas/kv-channel-protocol/versioned.ts following the same pattern as runner-protocol's versioned TypeScript
Generate or hand-write TypeScript types from v1.bare schema
Export serialize/deserialize functions for ToServer and ToClient message types
Include protocol version constant (PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the VBARE versioning pattern from engine/sdks/schemas/runner-protocol/. Check how runner-protocol/versioned.ts handles version embedding.

userStories.title: Add deleteRange to WASM VFS KvVfsOptions and migrate xTruncate/xDelete
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WASM VFS to use deleteRange for efficient O(1) chunk deletion instead of enumerating individual chunk keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Add deleteRange(start: Uint8Array, end: Uint8Array): Promise<void> to KvVfsOptions interface in rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-vfs/src/types.ts
Update xTruncate in vfs.ts to use deleteRange instead of deleteBatch for chunks beyond truncation point
Update #delete in vfs.ts to use deleteRange instead of enumerating chunk keys
The range for deleteRange should cover all chunk keys for the file tag being deleted (chunk keys are lexicographically contiguous due to fixed prefix + big-endian chunk index)
Update all implementations of KvVfsOptions to include deleteRange (check rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/db/shared.ts createActorKvStore function)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md lines 436-446 for migration plan. The runner protocol already has KvDeleteRangeRequest. The VFS chunk keys use [SQLITE_PREFIX, SCHEMA_VERSION, CHUNK_PREFIX, FILE_TAG, chunk_index_u32_be] layout, so a range from chunk 0 to chunk MAX covers all chunks for a file tag.

userStories.title: Add PRAGMA settings to WASM VFS open method
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WASM VFS to set PRAGMA busy_timeout and page_size after opening a database for KV-backed SQLite consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: After sqlite3.open_v2() in SqliteVfs.open() method in vfs.ts, execute PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000 and PRAGMA page_size = 4096
Add comment referencing native VFS (rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/vfs.rs) and spec doc for sync requirement
Remove or update the existing TODO comment about PRAGMA tuning
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md lines 414-429. busy_timeout=5000 matches the file-system driver. page_size=4096 matches CHUNK_SIZE. WAL mode is NOT enabled for KV-backed SQLite.

userStories.title: Create sqlite-native package scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the @rivetkit/sqlite-native package structure set up with Cargo.toml, package.json, build.rs, and platform-specific npm packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/ directory
Create Cargo.toml with napi-rs, libsqlite3-sys (bundled feature), tokio, tungstenite, serde, and bare-ts or serde-bare dependencies
Create package.json for @rivetkit/sqlite-native with napi-rs build scripts and optional dependencies on platform packages
Create build.rs for napi-rs
Create src/lib.rs with mod declarations for kv, protocol, channel, vfs (empty stub modules that compile)
Create src/kv.rs, src/protocol.rs, src/channel.rs, src/vfs.rs as empty modules with placeholder comments
Create npm/ directory with platform package.json files for: linux-x64-gnu, linux-arm64-gnu, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64, win32-x64-msvc
The crate must NOT be added to the root Cargo workspace (it has its own Cargo.toml with napi-rs deps)
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Rust Project Structure' section (lines 585-619). Follow napi-rs conventions. The crate statically links libsqlite3-sys with bundled feature. Platform packages follow @napi-rs/cli naming conventions.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native kv.rs (KV key layout)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Rust KV key layout module that produces byte-identical keys to the TypeScript implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement constants in kv.rs: CHUNK_SIZE=4096, SQLITE_PREFIX=0x08, SQLITE_SCHEMA_VERSION=0x01, META_PREFIX=0x00, CHUNK_PREFIX=0x01
Implement file tag constants: FILE_TAG_MAIN=0x00, FILE_TAG_JOURNAL=0x01, FILE_TAG_WAL=0x02, FILE_TAG_SHM=0x03
Implement get_meta_key(file_tag) -> [u8; 4] producing [SQLITE_PREFIX, SCHEMA_VERSION, META_PREFIX, file_tag]
Implement get_chunk_key(file_tag, chunk_index: u32) -> [u8; 8] producing [SQLITE_PREFIX, SCHEMA_VERSION, CHUNK_PREFIX, file_tag, chunk_index_bytes (big-endian)]
Add comments cross-referencing rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-vfs/src/kv.ts
Add #[cfg(test)] unit tests verifying byte-identical output to TypeScript implementation
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Key Layout' section (lines 388-406). Must produce byte-identical keys to kv.ts. Test with known inputs and compare against expected byte arrays from the TypeScript constants.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native protocol.rs (BARE serialization)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Rust types and BARE ser/de for all KV channel protocol messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Rust structs/enums for all v1.bare types: ToServer, ToClient, ToServerRequest, ToServerPong, ToClientResponse, ToClientPing, ToClientClose, RequestData, ResponseData, ErrorResponse, ActorOpenRequest, ActorCloseRequest, ActorOpenResponse, ActorCloseResponse, KvGetRequest, KvPutRequest, KvDeleteRequest, KvDeleteRangeRequest, KvGetResponse, KvPutResponse, KvDeleteResponse
Implement BARE serialization/deserialization for ToServer and ToClient unions
Use serde_bare or hand-written BARE encoding following the v1.bare schema exactly
Add round-trip tests for each message type
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See engine/sdks/schemas/kv-channel-protocol/v1.bare for the schema. BARE is a simple binary format. Consider using the bare-io or serde_bare crate, or hand-write the encoding if no suitable crate exists. Union types in BARE use a varint tag prefix.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native channel.rs (WebSocket KV channel client)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Rust WebSocket client that connects to the KV channel endpoint, sends requests, correlates responses by requestId, and reconnects on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement KvChannel struct with connect() that opens WebSocket to ws://{endpoint}/kv/connect?token={token}&namespace={ns}&protocol_version=1
Implement send_request(actor_id, request_data) -> ResponseData that serializes ToServerRequest, sends it, and waits for ToClientResponse with matching requestId
Implement requestId counter (u32, incrementing per request, resets to 0 on reconnect)
Implement reconnect with exponential backoff: initial=1000ms, max=30000ms, multiplier=2, jitter=0-25%
On reconnect, re-send ActorOpenRequest for all actors that were open before disconnect
On disconnect, fail all in-flight requests with connection error (do not retry automatically)
Handle ToClientPing by responding with ToServerPong
Handle ToClientClose by triggering reconnect
Use tokio for async WebSocket I/O with tungstenite
30-second timeout per KV operation (KV_EXPIRE = 30_000ms)
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Reconnect Behavior' (lines 487-508) and 'In-Flight Requests on Reconnect' (lines 500-508). The reconnect strategy matches the runner protocol (engine/sdks/typescript/runner/src/utils.ts). One WebSocket per process, shared across all actors.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native vfs.rs (custom SQLite VFS)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a custom SQLite VFS in Rust that maps VFS callbacks to KV operations via the channel.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement SQLite VFS using libsqlite3-sys raw C API (sqlite3_vfs, sqlite3_io_methods, sqlite3_file)
xRead: batch-fetch chunk keys via KvGetRequest, copy data to output buffer, handle partial chunks and short reads
xWrite: prefetch partial chunks if needed, batch-write via KvPutRequest, update metadata if size changed
xTruncate: KvDeleteRangeRequest for chunks beyond truncation point, optional partial chunk rewrite, update metadata
xDelete: KvDeleteRangeRequest for all chunks of file tag, KvDeleteRequest for metadata key
xSync: flush dirty metadata via KvPutRequest
xFileSize: read metadata key via KvGetRequest
xAccess: check metadata key existence via KvGetRequest
xLock/xUnlock: no-op (single-writer enforced by channel lock)
xOpen: register VFS scoped to actor, load existing metadata or create new file
xClose: flush dirty metadata, cleanup
Apply PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 and page_size=4096 after opening a database
Use same 4 KiB chunk layout and key encoding as kv.rs
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'VFS Callbacks' table (lines 433-443) and 'Custom VFS' section (lines 385-458). Each actor gets its own VFS instance parameterized by actor ID. Must use KvDeleteRangeRequest (not enumerate chunks) for xTruncate and xDelete. Add comment noting end-to-end tests are in driver-test-suite.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native lib.rs (napi-rs entry point)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the napi-rs bindings that expose connect, openDatabase, query, execute, closeDatabase, and disconnect to JavaScript.
acceptanceCriteria: Export connect(config: {url, token?, namespace}) -> KvChannel via napi-rs
Export openDatabase(channel, actorId) -> NativeDatabase that sends ActorOpenRequest optimistically
Export execute(db, sql, params?) -> {changes} for write statements
Export query(db, sql, params?) -> {rows, columns} for read statements
Export closeDatabase(db) that sends ActorCloseRequest and releases resources
Export disconnect(channel) that closes the WebSocket connection
Use #[napi] attribute macros for all exported functions and types
Tokio runtime is initialized once per process for async WebSocket I/O
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'JavaScript Bindings' section (lines 326-361). The API is minimal and synchronous from JS perspective (except connect/disconnect). SQLite operations block the N-API thread while waiting for KV responses via tokio.

userStories.title: Add KV channel WebSocket endpoint to manager router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the local dev manager to serve the KV channel WebSocket endpoint so native SQLite can connect during development.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WebSocket upgrade route at GET /kv/connect in rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/manager/router.ts
Accept query params: token, namespace, protocol_version
Validate token against config.token (RIVET_TOKEN). Skip auth with warning if not set in dev mode
Validate protocol_version=1, reject with HTTP error if unsupported
Deserialize incoming WebSocket binary messages as ToServer using BARE
Handle ActorOpenRequest: acquire single-writer lock per actorId, reject with actor_locked if held by another connection
Handle ActorCloseRequest: release lock for actorId
Handle KvGetRequest: read from actor KV storage, return KvGetResponse
Handle KvPutRequest: write to actor KV storage, return KvPutResponse
Handle KvDeleteRequest: delete from actor KV storage, return KvDeleteResponse
Handle KvDeleteRangeRequest: range-delete from actor KV storage, return KvDeleteResponse
Return ErrorResponse with appropriate error codes (actor_not_open, actor_locked, actor_not_found, key_too_large, etc.)
Send ToClientPing every 3 seconds, close connection if no ToServerPong within 15 seconds
On WebSocket disconnect, release all locks held by the connection
Serialize responses as ToClient using BARE
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Connection' (lines 70-74), 'Authentication' (lines 462-477), 'Single-Writer Lock Semantics' (lines 285-303), and 'Error Codes' (lines 248-264). The manager already has KV read endpoints at GET /actors/{actor_id}/kv/keys/{key} - reuse existing KV access patterns. KV limits from rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/drivers/file-system/kv-limits.ts must be enforced.

userStories.title: Create engine KV channel crate with WebSocket handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the production engine to serve the KV channel WebSocket endpoint for native SQLite in deployed environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new crate at engine/packages/kv-channel/ (or add to engine/packages/pegboard-runner/)
Add to workspace members in root Cargo.toml
Implement WebSocket handler at /kv/connect route
Accept query params: token, namespace, protocol_version
Validate token against auth.admin_token (RIVET__AUTH__ADMIN_TOKEN)
Validate protocol_version=1
Deserialize BARE messages (ToServer), serialize responses (ToClient)
Implement single-writer lock per actor (HashMap<ActorId, ConnectionId>)
Route KV operations to actor KV storage (reuse existing actor_kv module patterns from engine/packages/pegboard/src/actor_kv/mod.rs)
Enforce KV limits from engine/packages/pegboard/src/actor_kv/mod.rs

Handle all error codes from spec (actor_locked, actor_not_open, actor_not_found, key_too_large, value_too_large, payload_too_large, batch_too_large, storage_quota_exceeded, keys_values_length_mismatch, unauthorized, internal_error)
Ping every 3s, timeout if no pong within 15s (matching runner protocol defaults)
Send ToClientClose on graceful shutdown, finish in-flight responses before closing
Release all locks on WebSocket disconnect
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Authentication' (lines 462-477), 'Edge Cases' (lines 305-318), and 'KV Limits' (lines 266-281). Ping/pong intervals match runner_update_ping_interval_ms=3000 and runner_ping_timeout_ms=15000 from engine/packages/config/src/config/pegboard.rs.

userStories.title: Integrate native SQLite in database provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database provider to automatically use native SQLite when available and fall back to WASM.
acceptanceCriteria: Add nativeSqliteAvailable() function that attempts to load the @rivetkit/sqlite-native .node addon and catches all failure modes (missing file, glibc mismatch, N-API version mismatch, corrupted binary)
In rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/db/mod.ts, add conditional path: if native available, use nativeSqlite.openDatabase(); else log warning via pino and fall back to WASM
The Database interface exposed to actors (c.db.query(), c.db.execute()) must be identical regardless of backend
Warning message: 'native SQLite not available, falling back to WebAssembly. run npm rebuild to install native bindings.'
The KV channel connection (nativeSqlite.connect()) should be initialized once per process during startup, not per actor
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Integration with RivetKit' (lines 363-381). The KV channel is independent of the runner protocol and can be connected during process initialization. nativeSqliteAvailable() must try-catch the require() call, not just check file existence.

userStories.title: Update release scripts and version management for sqlite-native
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the release pipeline to correctly version and publish @rivetkit/sqlite-native and its platform packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add entry to scripts/release/update_version.ts for rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/package.json
Add entry for rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/npm/*/package.json (platform packages)
Add entry for rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/Cargo.toml version field
Verify getRivetkitPackages() in scripts/release/sdk.ts picks up sqlite-native and platform packages, or add them explicitly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Release Script Updates' (lines 668-706). The existing rivetkit-typescript/packages/*/package.json glob may cover the main package but npm/ subdirectories need explicit handling.

userStories.title: Add Rust VFS unit tests with mock WebSocket server
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the native VFS to validate chunk mapping, key encoding, error handling, and reconnection without requiring a real server.
acceptanceCriteria: Add #[cfg(test)] module in rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/ (can be in vfs.rs or a dedicated test module)
Implement mock WebSocket server that runs in-process with in-memory KV store
Test xRead/xWrite correctly map byte ranges to 4 KiB KV chunks
Test chunk boundary handling (partial reads/writes spanning chunks)
Test xTruncate and xDelete produce correct KV delete ranges
Test metadata (file size) persistence via xSync/xClose
Test file tags (main, journal) key encoding
Test error handling (actor not open, actor locked)
Test optimistic open pipelining (open + immediate KV requests)
Test reconnection (mock server drops connection, client reconnects and re-opens actors)
Add comment noting end-to-end tests are in driver-test-suite (rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/driver-test-suite/)
cargo test passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Testing' section (lines 548-579). Layer 1 tests only. Layer 2 (driver test suite) already exists and will test native SQLite transparently when the addon is available.
